title: PeeHD – A Smart Urinals Concept
author: Choudhary, Abhishek
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Abstract 
 The PeeHD (Precision Health Diagnostics) system reimagines the standard urinal as a high-fidelity biological data node for continuous health monitoring. By embedding real-time uro-analysis capabilities directly into the sanitation infrastructure, PeeHD enables passive, non-invasive, and clinically robust data acquisition. This integration supports both individual diagnostics and population-scale health surveillance within a PoLPEST-informed framework. 
  --- 
 System Architecture 
 1. Sample Capture: Sample Isolation Gate (SIG) 
 Electromechanical valve captures a 5–10 mL mid-stream aliquot, reducing contamination risks. 
 Ensures integrity comparable to clinically recommended “clean catch” procedures. 
 Excess fluid diverted into the standard drainage system. 
  2. Sample Preparation: Microfluidic Analysis Core (MAC) 
 Houses a modular Microfluidic Cartridge (MFC) (disposable/semi-permanent). 
 Functions: controlled micro-pumping, buffer dilution, reagent introduction. 
 Microchannels (50–200 µm) allow rapid and precise chemical reactions. 
  3. Analysis: Spectroscopic Sensor Array (SSA) 
 Multi-modal optical detection: UV-Vis, optional Raman spectroscopy. 
 Measures both reaction outputs and direct physical parameters (specific gravity, turbidity). 
 Additional probes: conductivity, pH, temperature for normalization and robustness. 
  4. Processing: Embedded Processor (EP) 
 Low-power edge-computing unit for sensor data acquisition. 
 Runs calibration and BioMarker Pattern Recognition (BPR) AI models. 
 Enables anomaly detection against both population-level baselines and personal longitudinal data. 
  5. User Feedback: OLED Interface 
 High-resolution, non-touch OLED display. 
 Linguistic Equity design: supports complex scripts (Devanagari, Nastaliq, etc.) and universal graphical cues. 
 Balances transparency and clarity—provides direct actionable insights (e.g., “Healthy”, “Consult Physician”). 
  6. Connectivity & Data Integration 
 Secure Communications Module: Wi-Fi / BLE 5.0 with AES-256 + TLS 1.3. 
 Outputs standardized data streams compliant with HL7 FHIR. 
 Enables integration with Health Data Lakes or Personal Health Records (PHR). 
   
 --- 
 Core Principles 
 Passive & Non-invasive: Converts routine urination into a diagnostic act. 
 Clinical Integrity: Maintains sample quality through precision isolation. 
 Scalable Public Health Utility: Supports population-scale monitoring within smart-city infrastructure.
